food_subgroup: Stir-fried rice with sweet soy sauce and egg (Indonesian style)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Nasi Goreng. Nearest parent is a fried rice dish. A new FoodOn concept is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite rice dishes — stir-fried rice with sweet soy sauce and egg (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical plate of Indonesian Nasi Goreng weighs approximately 350g and consists of day-old jasmine rice stir-fried with kecap manis (sweet soy sauce), shallots, garlic, and chilli, topped with a fried egg, and accompanied by cucumber slices, tomato, and prawn crackers.

nutrient_source.title: Nasi Goreng — FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table; HPB Singapore Foodo Food Code F05100083
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table; Health Promotion Board (HPB), Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match found in HPB Foodo under food code F05100083 — Nasi Goreng, Indonesian hawker style. Cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA Indonesian food data.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — white rice (fried); similar GI to plain white rice; kecap manis adds sugar but does not significantly alter the overall GI relative to plain fried rice.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 1813mg per serving (350g) — about 79% of the 2300mg daily recommended limit, contributed by kecap manis, soy sauce, and seasoning.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced kecap manis and soy sauce; nasi goreng contains less sodium than many other hawker rice dishes due to lighter sauce usage compared to Chinese fried rice.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Jasmine rice base has a high GI (~70); kecap manis adds approximately 4.8g sugar per 100g; per-serving glycaemic load of approximately 59 is high.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should limit nasi goreng to a smaller portion (200g) and request reduced kecap manis; the added sugar from the sweet soy sauce compounds the already high glycaemic impact of the rice base.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Shallots and garlic in the stir-fry provide quercetin and allicin with anti-inflammatory effects; the egg provides selenium and vitamin D; overall moderate fat profile.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Nasi Goreng is an acceptable meal choice for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the shallot and garlic base provides anti-inflammatory compounds, and requesting extra vegetables as a side improves the overall anti-inflammatory profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nasi Goreng is Indonesia's beloved national fried rice dish — day-old jasmine rice stir-fried in a hot wok with shallots, garlic, and bird's eye chilli, seasoned with kecap manis (sweet Indonesian soy sauce) that gives the rice a characteristic deep brown colour and subtly sweet flavour. The dish is topped with a fried egg and served with cucumber slices, tomato wedges, and often prawn crackers. Unlike Chinese fried rice, Nasi Goreng is defined by the kecap manis sauce and the aromatic shallot-garlic-chilli base, making it sweeter and more fragrant.

confidence_reason: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo under food code F05100083 — Nasi Goreng, Indonesian hawker style; cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA.
notes: Halal in standard Indonesian/Malay preparation (no pork). The sugar content from kecap manis is clinically significant for diabetic patients. Prawn crackers accompaniment adds approximately 50–70 kcal per serving if consumed.
